metadata.summary: Production voice AI agents on STT → LLM → TTS pipelines. Retell, Vapi, ElevenLabs for phone/web. Latency target 600-800ms. Keep responses 1-3 sentences, strip markdown.

content.title: Voice Agents
content.summary: Production voice AI agents built on real-time STT → LLM → TTS pipelines. Use managed platforms (Retell AI, Vapi, ElevenLabs) for phone and web voice agents. Total pipeline latency target is 600-800ms; anything above 1.5s feels broken. Keep LLM responses to 1-3 sentences and strip all markdown before TTS synthesis.

why.p: Voice agents replace IVR menus and phone support with natural conversational AI. The market is growing at 22.7% CAGR. Managed platforms absorb the hardest infrastructure problems (WebRTC, VAD, barge-in, telephony integration) so that teams can focus on conversation design and tool integration. Building the media layer from scratch (LiveKit) is only justified when self-hosting is required or latency requirements are below 300ms.

list.item: Building phone-based AI agents: inbound/outbound call center, appointment scheduling, support
Web or mobile app requires real-time voice conversation (browser WebRTC)
Replacing IVR (Interactive Voice Response) menus with natural conversational AI
Voice accessibility feature: hands-free interface for existing agent workflows
Live translation agent: real-time speech-to-speech across 100+ languages
Text chat is sufficient — voice adds latency, cost, and infrastructure complexity for no UX gain
Latency budget is below 300ms end-to-end — no current production stack achieves this reliably
Audio quality is unpredictable (noisy environment, poor microphone) — accuracy degrades significantly
Use case requires precise multi-step confirmation (legal, medical consent) — voice ambiguity introduces risk; use structured text forms instead
Self-hosted required with <$500/month infra budget — LiveKit self-hosting requires real-time media server expertise

section.name: Voice Agent Platforms: Selection and Comparison

rule.statement: Use Retell AI or Vapi for phone-based agents when time-to-production is the priority. Use ElevenLabs Conversational AI when voice quality is the primary differentiator. Use LiveKit only when self-hosted infrastructure is required.
rule.rationale: Managed platforms absorb the hardest problems: WebRTC setup, VAD tuning, telephony integration, billing per minute. LiveKit self-hosting requires real-time media server expertise and adds operational burden that is rarely worth it unless data residency requirements demand it.

list.item: Phone agents (inbound/outbound): Retell AI ($0.07-0.12/min) or Vapi ($0.05-0.10/min)
Premium voice quality: ElevenLabs Conversational AI (~500ms latency, $0.50/min)
Self-hosted required: LiveKit (OSS, WebRTC media server)
Rapid prototyping: Vapi (good webhook support, web playground)
Outbound campaigns: Bland.ai ($0.09/min, bulk scheduling)
Enterprise low latency: Synthflow (<100ms, custom pricing)

rule.statement: Use WebRTC for browser and mobile apps (lowest latency). Use SIP/PSTN for telephony integration. Budget for 100-200ms additional latency on PSTN calls vs WebRTC.
rule.rationale: PSTN adds carrier-grade latency on top of the AI pipeline. For real-time conversational feel, WebRTC is preferred where available. SIP trunking via Twilio or Retell bridges the gap for phone number requirements.

section.p: Total pipeline (STT + LLM + TTS) must stay under 600-800ms for natural conversation. Component targets: STT (Speech-to-Text): <200ms; VAD (Voice Activity Detection): <50ms; LLM inference: <500ms (keep responses to 1-3 sentences); TTS synthesis: <200ms; Turn detection: <100ms.

antipattern.description: Using GPT-4o Realtime API (speech-to-speech) expecting tool-call support during streaming.
antipattern.reason: GPT-4o Realtime API has no tool-call support during streaming as of 2026. The model must finish speaking before tools execute, adding a full turn of latency. Use STT → LLM → TTS pipeline for tool-heavy voice agents.

section.name: Voice Agent Pipeline: Configuration and Conversation Design

rule.statement: Keep LLM responses to 1-3 sentences. Strip all markdown, bullet points, and special characters from LLM output before TTS synthesis. Explicitly forbid markdown in the system prompt.
rule.rationale: TTS systems read markdown literally: "**bold text**" becomes "asterisk asterisk bold text asterisk asterisk". Long responses increase TTS latency and feel unnatural in conversation. Users tolerate 3-4 seconds of silence before interpreting it as a disconnect.

example.title: Voice agent system prompt with markdown prohibition
example.code:
VOICE_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are a customer support agent for Acme Corp.
Keep ALL responses to 1-2 sentences maximum. Never use bullet points, numbered lists, or markdown formatting. Never use asterisks, hashtags, or special characters. When checking information, say "Let me look that up for you" while the tool runs. If unsure, ask one clarifying question at a time. """

antipattern.description: Not explicitly forbidding markdown in the voice agent system prompt.
antipattern.reason: LLMs generate markdown formatting by default even when not instructed to. Without an explicit prohibition, roughly 20-30% of responses will contain formatting characters that TTS reads as literal text, breaking the natural conversation flow.

rule.statement: Implement barge-in: detect user speech during agent TTS output and interrupt immediately. Set endpointing_delay to 400-600ms to prevent VAD false positives from background noise triggering premature turn completion.
rule.rationale: Without barge-in, users must wait for the full agent response before speaking, which feels robotic. Without proper endpointing delay, background noise causes the agent to respond mid-user-sentence. Both destroy conversation naturalness.

rule.statement: Use filler phrases ("Let me check that for you", "One moment please") while tool calls execute. Tool calls must complete in under 2 seconds or the agent sounds unresponsive. Cache frequent lookups (order status, user data).
rule.rationale: Silence exceeding 1 second in a phone conversation is perceived as a disconnect or technical failure. Filler phrases acknowledge the user's request while the tool executes, maintaining conversational continuity.

example.title: Tool call pattern with filler phrase

rule.statement: Set a conversation timeout of 15-20 minutes to prevent runaway agent sessions. Log full transcripts with turn latencies per call. For outbound campaigns, implement TCPA/GDPR consent workflows before any autodial.
rule.rationale: Telephony is billed per minute — runaway sessions consume budget without user interaction. TCPA violations in the US carry $500-$1,500 per call fines. Consent workflows are non-negotiable for outbound.

section.name: Voice Agents: Production Checklist and Monitoring
section.p: Pre-launch checklist: Test with real phone numbers and real accents — simulator accuracy differs from production. Measure end-to-end latency p95 across all pipeline components. Test barge-in with background noise and concurrent speakers. Verify tool call latency under 2 seconds for all integrations. Test escalation flow to human agent. Configure call recording consent disclosure (if recording). Set conversation timeout (15-20 minutes maximum). Secure all API keys in environment variables.

rule.statement: Track average handle time, task completion rate, escalation rate, and per-component latency in production. p95 latency per component reveals where to optimize.
rule.rationale: Voice agent quality degrades gradually as conversation flows evolve. Without per-call metrics, regressions are invisible until user complaints surface. Weekly review of conversation transcripts and latency distributions enables proactive optimization.

section.p: Monitoring metrics: Average handle time (target: task-dependent, typically 3-7 minutes for support). Task completion rate (target: >70% for well-designed flows). Escalation rate (target: <20%; high rate indicates prompt or flow issues). p95 turn latency broken down: STT / LLM / TTS / tool call. Call recording consent rate (outbound only).

rule.statement: Budget for per-minute telephony billing (Retell, Vapi, Bland) — not per-LLM-token — when using managed platforms. A 5-minute call at $0.09/min costs $0.45 regardless of how many turns it takes.
rule.rationale: Voice platform cost models differ fundamentally from text API cost models. Teams that budget using token estimates significantly undershoot actual costs for telephony-based deployments.

antipattern.description: Autodialing for outbound campaigns without TCPA/GDPR consent workflow.
antipattern.reason: TCPA (US) fines are $500-$1,500 per unconsented call. GDPR consent is required for EU citizens. Implement explicit consent collection and storage before any outbound campaign, and verify consent records are accessible to the agent for audits.

section.name: Agentic Workflow
section.p: Voice agents are real-time event-driven pipelines, not request-response agents. Audio arrives in 100-200ms chunks from WebRTC/WebSocket; each chunk flows through VAD → STT → LLM → TTS and back as audio. An orchestrator subagent maintains conversation state (history, context, tool results) and is invoked on each user turn completion (detected by VAD/turn detection). Tool calls happen between turns: the agent pauses TTS synthesis, executes the tool (CRM lookup, calendar booking), then resumes. Use managed platforms (Retell, Vapi) to avoid building the media layer; build custom only for LiveKit self-hosted deployments.
Recommended subagents: `faion-sdd-executor-agent` — can design the conversation flow and tool integration for a voice agent pipeline.

section.name: Best Practices and Gotchas

list.item: Keep LLM response length to 1-3 sentences — long responses are unnatural over voice and increase TTS latency
Strip markdown, bullet points, and special characters from LLM output before TTS synthesis
Implement barge-in: detect user speech during agent TTS output and interrupt immediately via VAD
Use filler phrases ("Let me check that for you") while tool calls execute — silence >1s feels like a disconnect
Log full transcripts + turn latencies per call; p95 latency per component reveals where to optimize
Set a conversation timeout (15-20 mins) to prevent runaway agent sessions consuming telephony minutes
Test with real phone numbers and real accents before launch — simulator accuracy differs significantly from production
Use webhooks for post-call processing (CRM updates, quality scoring) rather than blocking the call thread

section.p: Gotchas: Voice system prompts must explicitly forbid markdown — LLMs generate `**bold**` text even when not instructed to. Tool calls during voice must complete in <2s or the agent sounds unresponsive; cache frequent lookups (order status, user data). GPT-4o Realtime API requires WebRTC peer connection — cannot be called from a standard Python async context without a media bridge. Retell/Vapi charge per minute of call time, not per LLM token — cost model is different from text agents; budget accordingly. PSTN calls add ~150ms latency vs WebRTC; budget for this in latency requirements. VAD false positives (background noise triggers turn detection) cause the agent to respond mid-user-sentence; tune `endpointing_delay` to 400-600ms. Outbound campaigns via Bland/Retell require compliance with TCPA (US) and GDPR — do not autodial without explicit consent workflows.

section.name: Tools and Services

list.item: livekit-agents: Self-hosted voice agent framework (Python) | `pip install livekit-agents` | [docs](https://docs.livekit.io/agents/)
retell-sdk: Retell AI Python/JS SDK | `pip install retell-sdk` | [docs](https://docs.retellai.com/)
vapi-python: Vapi Python SDK | `pip install vapi-python` | [docs](https://docs.vapi.ai/)
twilio: PSTN/SIP telephony integration | `pip install twilio` | [docs](https://www.twilio.com/docs)
openai: GPT-4o Realtime API (WebRTC/WebSocket) | `pip install openai` | [realtime docs](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/realtime)
elevenlabs: ElevenLabs TTS + Conversational AI SDK | `pip install elevenlabs` | [docs](https://elevenlabs.io/docs)
ffmpeg: Audio format conversion for telephony | system package

section.p: Retell AI: SaaS, Best for phone agents, $0.07-0.12/min, handles full pipeline.
Vapi: SaaS, Rapid prototyping, $0.05-0.10/min, good webhook support.
ElevenLabs Conversational AI: SaaS, ~500ms latency, premium voice quality, $0.50/min.
LiveKit: OSS + SaaS, Self-hosted or cloud, WebRTC media server, most flexible.
Bland.ai: SaaS, Outbound campaigns, $0.09/min, bulk scheduling.
OpenAI Realtime API: SaaS, Speech-to-speech, 128K context, no streaming tool calls.
Twilio ConversationRelay: SaaS, PSTN bridge for any AI backend, pairs with LiveKit/Retell.
Synthflow: SaaS, Enterprise telephony, <100ms latency, custom pricing.

template.purpose: Minimal Retell AI webhook handler (FastAPI, ~45 lines)
Voice agent system prompt template — short responses, no markdown, tool call pattern
